Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. 2026-03-19T02:44:00.5369323Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-19T02:43:17.670013496Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57231,\"title\":\"[Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding] UpdateAnalyzer/UpdateAnalyzerAsync docs should clarify which properties are updatable\",\"body\":\"## Description\\n\\nThe `UpdateAnalyzer` and `UpdateAnalyzerAsync` methods (and their protocol overloads) currently have XML doc summaries that say only \\u0026#34;Update analyzer properties\\u0026#34; without specifying **which** properties can actually be updated.\\n\\nThe underlying REST API uses `PATCH` with `application/merge-patch+json`, and **only `description` and `tags`** are accepted by the service. All other properties (`models`, `fieldSchema`, `config`, `baseAnalyzerId`, `dynamicFieldSchema`, `processingLocation`, `knowledgeSources`) are create-only — they are silently ignored by the server even though it returns HTTP 200.\\n\\nThis has caused customer confusion — see #57215.\\n\\n## Requested Improvements\\n\\n1. **API doc (`` / ``)**: Add a `` section to `UpdateAnalyzer`/`UpdateAnalyzerAsync` listing the updatable properties and noting that other properties require `CreateAnalyzerAsync` with `allowReplace: true`.\\n\\n2. **Sample doc (Sample08_UpdateAnalyzer.md)**: Add a note in the \\u0026#34;About updating analyzers\\u0026#34; section explaining that only `description` and `tags` are updatable via this method, and reference `CreateAnalyzerAsync` with `allowReplace: true` for changing `models`, `fieldSchema`, etc.\\n\\n## Updatable Properties\\n\\n| Property | Updatable via `UpdateAnalyzer` (PATCH)? |\\n|---|---|\\n| `description` | **Yes** |\\n| `tags` | **Yes** |\\n| `models` | No — silently ignored |\\n| `fieldSchema` | No — silently ignored |\\n| `config` | No — silently ignored |\\n| `baseAnalyzerId` | No — silently ignored |\\n| `dynamicFieldSchema` | No — silently ignored |\\n| `processingLocation` | No — silently ignored |\\n| `knowledgeSources` | No — silently ignored |\\n\\n## Workaround\\n\\nTo change create-only properties on an existing analyzer, use `CreateAnalyzerAsync` with `allowReplace: true`. This calls `PUT` (full resource replacement) instead of `PATCH`.\\n\\n## Related\\n\\n- #57215 — customer report of `UpdateAnalyzerAsync` not updating `models`\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57231\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"chienyuanchang\",\"id\":186005646,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/chienyuanchang\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/186005646?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"Cognitive - Content Understanding\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-03-19T02:39:39Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-19T02:39:39Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

2026-03-19T02:44:00.5497332Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-19T02:43:27.11586907Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"issues\":[{\"number\":57231,\"title\":\"[Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding] UpdateAnalyzer/UpdateAnalyzerAsync docs should clarify which properties are updatable\",\"body\":\"## Description\\n\\nThe `UpdateAnalyzer` and `UpdateAnalyzerAsync` methods (and their protocol overloads) currently have XML doc summaries that say only \\u0026#34;Update analyzer properties\\u0026#34; without specifying **which** properties can actually be updated.\\n\\nThe underlying REST API uses `PATCH` with `application/merge-patch+json`, and **only `description` and `tags`** are accepted by the service. All other properties (`models`, `fieldSchema`, `config`, `baseAnalyzerId`, `dynamicFieldSchema`, `processingLocation`, `knowledgeSources`) are create-only — they are silently ignored by the server even though it returns HTTP 200.\\n\\nThis has caused customer confusion — see #57215.\\n\\n## Requested Improvements\\n\\n1. **API doc (`` / ``)**: Add a `` section to `UpdateAnalyzer`/`UpdateAnalyzerAsync` listing the updatable properties and noting that other properties require `CreateAnalyzerAsync` with `allowReplace: true`.\\n\\n2. **Sample doc (Sample08_UpdateAnalyzer.md)**: Add a note in the \\u0026#34;About updating analyzers\\u0026#34; section explaining that only `description` and `tags` are updatable via this method, and reference `CreateAnalyzerAsync` with `allowReplace: true` for changing `models`, `fieldSchema`, etc.\\n\\n## Updatable Properties\\n\\n| Property | Updatable via `UpdateAnalyzer` (PATCH)? |\\n|---|---|\\n| `description` | **Yes** |\\n| `tags` | **Yes** |\\n| `models` | No — silently ignored |\\n| `fieldSchema` | No — silently ignored |\\n| `config` | No — silently ignored |\\n| `baseAnalyzerId` | No — silently ignored |\\n| `dynamicFieldSchema` | No — silently ignored |\\n| `processingLocation` | No — silently ignored |\\n| `knowledgeSources` | No — silently ignored |\\n\\n## Workaround\\n\\nTo change create-only properties on an existing analyzer, use `CreateAnalyzerAsync` with `allowReplace: true`. This calls `PUT` (full resource replacement) instead of `PATCH`.\\n\\n## Related\\n\\n- #57215 — customer report of `UpdateAnalyzerAsync` not updating `models`\\n\",\"state\":\"OPEN\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"chienyuanchang\"},\"labels\":[\"Cognitive - Content Understanding\"],\"created_at\":\"2026-03-19T02:39:39Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-19T02:39:39Z\"},{\"number\":57212,\"title\":\"[provisioning] clean up redundant `Directory.Build.props` for provisioning libraries\",\"body\":\"Provisioning libraries now have been moved into each serivce\\u0026#39;s serivce directory.\\nBut they all have a complicated Directory.Build.props, for example: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/datafactory/Azure.Provisioning.DataFactory/Directory.Build.props\\n\\nWe should find a place to store some common properties, so that this file can be simplified.\",\"state\":\"OPEN\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"ArcturusZhang\"},\"labels\":[\"EngSys\",\"Provisioning\"],\"created_at\":\"2026-03-18T09:42:25Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-18T09:42:25Z\"},{\"number\":57205,\"title\":\"[Provisioning] Implement a new serialization system for BicepTypes\",\"body\":\"It would be nice if all the bicep types could use IPersistableModel to support serialization into bicep snippets and aspire RPCs.\\n\\nMore details will be updated here.\",\"state\":\"OPEN\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"ArcturusZhang\"},\"labels\":[\"CodeGen\",\"Provisioning\"],\"created_at\":\"2026-03-18T03:07:39Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-18T03:07:54Z\"},{\"number\":57184,\"title\":\"Explore TypeForwarding Azure.Identity types to Azure.Core to enable per-library AddFooClient extensions\",\"body\":\"## Problem\\n\\nIndividual SDK libraries (e.g., `Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets`) need to provide `AddFooClient` DI extension methods that call `WithAzureCredential` to enable the full configuration and dependency injection experience described in #55491. Currently, `WithAzureCredential` and its supporting types (`ConfigurableCredential`, `ConfigurableCredentialCache`, `DefaultAzureCredentialOptions`) are defined in `Azure.Identity`. This means each SDK library would need to take a **new direct dependency on Azure.Identity** to offer these extensions — adding a dependency to every SDK package that doesn\\u0026#39;t currently have one.\\n\\n## Proposed Approach to Explore\\n\\nSince all SDK libraries already depend on `Azure.Core`, and `Azure.Identity` already depends on `Azure.Core`, we should explore whether we can use [TypeForwardedTo](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.runtime.compilerservices.typeforwardedtoattribute) to make the relevant types available through `Azure.Core` without requiring a direct `Azure.Identity` dependency.\\n\\nThe high-level idea:\\n1. **Move** the types needed by `WithAzureCredential` from `Azure.Identity` into `Azure.Core`\\n2. **Add** `[assembly: TypeForwardedTo(...)]` attributes in `Azure.Identity` pointing to the new locations in `Azure.Core`\\n3. SDK libraries can then reference these types through their existing `Azure.Core` dependency\\n4. **Existing consumers of Azure.Identity are unaffected** — the runtime follows type forwarding transparently, so no breaking change occurs\\n\\nThis should not break any existing users of `Azure.Identity` since:\\n- `TypeForwardedTo` is a well-established .NET mechanism for moving types across assemblies\\n- `Azure.Core` is already in every SDK\\u0026#39;s dependency chain\\n- Existing compiled code referencing these types via `Azure.Identity` will be redirected at runtime\\n\\n## Types That May Need to Move\\n\\nBased on the current `WithAzureCredential` implementation in `Azure.Identity/src/ConfigurationExtensions.cs`, the following types are referenced:\\n\\n| Type | Current Location | Notes |\\n|------|-----------------|-------|\\n| `ConfigurableCredential` | Azure.Identity (currently internal) | Extends `TokenCredential`; visibility may need adjustment |\\n| `ConfigurableCredentialCache` | Azure.Identity | Static caching layer for credential reuse |\\n| `DefaultAzureCredentialOptions` | Azure.Identity | Options class for credential configuration |\\n| `WithAzureCredential` extensions | Azure.Identity (`ConfigurationExtensions`) | The extension methods on `ClientSettings` and `IClientBuilder` |\\n\\n## Open Questions and Risks\\n\\n- **Internal types**: `ConfigurableCredential` is currently `internal` — `TypeForwardedTo` only works with public types, so the API surface may need to be adjusted or an alternative abstraction introduced\\n- **Transitive dependency concerns**: `ConfigurableCredential` has deep dependencies on MSAL libraries, `DefaultAzureCredential`, `ChainedTokenCredential`, etc. — moving it directly to `Azure.Core` could pull in unwanted transitive dependencies unless the design is refactored (e.g., using an interface/abstraction in Azure.Core that Azure.Identity implements)\\n- **Alternative patterns**: Instead of moving concrete types, it may be more appropriate to define an abstraction or factory pattern in Azure.Core that Azure.Identity fulfills at runtime\\n- **Existing precedent**: `TypeForwardedTo` is rarely used in this repo today (only 1 instance found in `Azure.Messaging.EventGrid`), so this would establish a new pattern\\n\\n## Goal\\n\\nEnable SDK libraries to provide `AddFooClient` extension methods with full `WithAzureCredential` support **without adding a new direct dependency on Azure.Identity**. This issue tracks the investigation of TypeForwarding as the primary approach, but alternative solutions may emerge during exploration.\",\"state\":\"OPEN\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"m-nash\"},\"labels\":[\"Client\",\"Azure.Core\",\"Azure.Identity\"],\"comments\":1,\"created_at\":\"2026-03-17T18:53:04Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-18T00:04:52Z\"},{\"number\":57183,\"title\":\"[BUG] Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent NullReferenceException after successful streamed response finalization\",\"body\":\"## Bug description\\nWhen using `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` through Microsoft Agent Framework streaming APIs, a chat run can complete successfully, return the full assistant answer, and then crash during stream finalization/disposal with a `System.NullReferenceException`.\\n\\nThe exception originates inside the Azure SDK telemetry/finalization path:\\n`Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.JsonModelWriteCore(...)`\\n\\n## Package and environment\\n- Package: `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent`\\n- Package version: `1.0.0-preview.260311.1`\\n- App shape: .NET 10 console app using Microsoft Agent Framework with Azure Foundry persistent agents\\n- OS: Windows\\n- Auth=[REDACTED] Streaming path: `AIAgent.RunStreamingAsync(...)`\\n\\n## Expected behavior\\nAfter the streamed answer completes, enumeration/disposal should finish cleanly.\\n\\n## Actual behavior\\nThe full assistant answer is returned to the caller, but the process crashes afterward during stream cleanup/final telemetry processing.\\n\\n## Reproduction notes\\n1. Create a persistent Foundry agent and connect with `PersistentAgentsClient` / `ChatClientAgent`.\\n2. Wrap the agent with Agent Framework OpenTelemetry instrumentation.\\n3. Call `RunStreamingAsync(...)` and enumerate the response stream to completion.\\n4. Observe that, after the final assistant output is emitted, the SDK throws `NullReferenceException` during finalization.\\n\\n## Minimal observed output\\n```text\\nassistant(stream)\\u0026gt; \\n[update] role=assistant, messageId=..., author=..., responseId=...\\n[update] role=assistant, messageId=..., author=..., responseId=...\\n[content:UsageContent] Microsoft.Extensions.AI.UsageContent\\nUnhandled exception. System.NullReferenceException: Object reference not set to an instance of an object.\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.JsonModelWriteCore(Utf8JsonWriter writer, ModelReaderWriterOptions options)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.System.ClientModel.Primitives.IJsonModel.Write(...)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.ModelSerializationExtensions.WriteObjectValue[T](...)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.ToRequestContent()\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.GetToolCallAttributes(RunStepToolCall toolCall)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.ProcessToolCalls(RunStepToolCallDetails toolCallDetails)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.RecordRunStepEventAttributes(RunStep runStep, Boolean stream)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.End()\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.Dispose()\\n```\\n\\n## Suspected root cause\\nThe generated serializer for `RunStepOpenAPIToolCall` appears to iterate `OpenAPI` without a null check.\\n\\nRelevant file: `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/src/Generated/RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.Serialization.cs`\\n\\n## Impact\\n- The user-visible answer is already complete, but the client process still terminates.\\n- This makes streamed chat runs unreliable in production.\\n- The issue appears tied to SDK finalization/telemetry, not application rendering.\\n\\n## Workaround currently used\\nApplication-side workaround: catch this very specific `NullReferenceException` after the stream completes and preserve the completed answer.\\n\\n## Request\\nPlease confirm whether this is a known bug in `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` preview and whether a null-safe fix should be added around `RunStepOpenAPIToolCall` serialization / telemetry attribute generation.\",\"state\":\"OPEN\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"brandonh-msft\"},\"labels\":[\"Service Attention\",\"Client\",\"customer-reported\",\"needs-team-attention\",\"AI Agents\"],\"created_at\":\"2026-03-17T18:52:04Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-17T21:07:17Z\"}],\"totalCount\":990,\"pageInfo\":{\"hasNextPage\":true,\"hasPreviousPage\":false,\"startCursor\":\"Y3Vyc29yOnYyOpK0MjAyNi0wMy0xOVQwMjozOTozOVrO9E_-pw==\",\"endCursor\":\"Y3Vyc29yOnYyOpK0MjAyNi0wMy0xN1QxODo1MjowNFrO89BWGQ==\"}}","type":"text"}]}}}
